identificationModule.briefTitle: Positive Airway Pressure Therapy Study in Sleep Apnea and Diastolic Heart Failure
identificationModule.officialTitle: Positive Airway Pressure Therapy Study in Sleep Apnea and Diastolic Heart Failure

descriptionModule.briefSummary: This is a multicenter, prospective, randomized clinical trial (pilot study). 20 participants with diastolic heart failure will be assigned to ventilation therapy (AutoSet CS ™ ASV device; therapy group). The trial will run for 6 months, after which change in echocardiographic parameters of diastolic left ventricular function, sleep and quality of life parameters will be evaluated.

armGroups.label: Adaptive servo ventilation (ASV)
armGroups.description: This group will receive ventilation therapy (AutoSet CS, ASV device)

interventions.name: Adaptive servo ventilation (ASV)
interventions.description: Non-invasive Ventilation used to control central and obstructive sleep apnea.

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Change in total sleeping time (TST) determined by parameters of polysomnography
secondaryOutcomes.description: Total sleeping time (TST) will be measured in minutes (min).

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Change in sleep quality determined by parameters of polysomnography
secondaryOutcomes.description: Sleep Quality will be defined by sleep architecture (time spent in sleep Phase 1,2,3 and REM).

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Change in sleep efficacy determined by parameters of polysomnography
secondaryOutcomes.description: Sleep efficacy is defined as TST/total time spent in bed, i.e. sleep/awake periods.

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Change of echocardiographic parameter of diastolic left ventricular ejection fraction function E/è
secondaryOutcomes.description: E (mitral inflow velocity) and è (mitral annular early diastolic velocity) are parameters to measure filling of the left ventricle with blood.The E/è Ratio gives an estimation of the filling pressure of the left ventricle.

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Change of echocardiographic parameters of diastolic function

secondaryOutcomes.description: ESS (Epworth Sleepiness Scale) estimates the sleepiness of a person during daytime.
MLHFQ (Minnesota Living with Heart Failure) estimates how much HF impairs the daily life of a patient.
PHQ9 (Patient health questionnaire) assesses depression of a patient.
SF12HSQ (short form health status) assesses physical and mental health status.

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Change in NT-pro BNP and BNP
secondaryOutcomes.description: NT-pro BNP and BNP (unit ng/l) are comprehensive protein markers of heart diseases, inflammations and cardiovascular disorders. Outcome: Assessment of heart disease status.
Reasons will be described in the corresponding files: Type of disease: Treatment related or unrelated?
Length of hospitalizations are measured in days (d). Severity of disease.

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Occurrence of Adverse Events (AE) and Serious Adverse Events (SAE)
Changes in parameters of cardiac repolarisation (QT(c), TpTe(c) intervals, TpTe/QT ratio) in ECG at rest
secondaryOutcomes.description: Electrocardiogram parameters are being put together to assess the patient's heart function status. Outcome: Number of patients with normal heart function.

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Changes in occurrence of ventricular and supraventricular arrhythmias (PSG-ECG)
secondaryOutcomes.description: Parameters are being put together to assess the patient's heart function status. Outcome: Number of patients with arrhythmias.

eligibilityModule.eligibilityCriteria:
Inclusion Criteria:

* Clinical signs and symptoms of heart failure according to the ESC guidelines
* Echocardiographic evidence of a preserved systolic left ventricular function (echocardiographic left ventricular ejection fraction \> 50%)
* Polysomnographic evidence of at least moderate sleep-disordered breathing: apnea-hypopnea index (AHI) \> 15 / h with ≥ 50% central events and with with a central AHI of ≥ 10/h (AHI based on total sleeping time, TST)
* BNP or NT-pro BNP ≥ 300 pg / ml
* NYHA class II or III
* Patient tolerates the ventilation treatment (1h sample ventilation)
* Stable drug therapy in the last 4 weeks prior to randomization (no iv diuretic therapy or increasing the dose of diuretics to ≥ 100%)
* Minimum age of patients of 18 years
* patient willing to undergo study procedures
* signed informed consent

Exclusion Criteria:

* Oxygen saturation (sO2) \<92% of the days in peace without O2 therapy, measured baseline (start time) in the pulse oximetry or polysomnography in a cape. BGA (the measurement must be max. Lie back 1 week)
* Patients, who have a risk professional with symptomatic sleep apnea
* Known COPD with FEV1 \<70%
* patients with NYHA stadium IV
* Advanced congenital heart defects
* pericardial diseases
* Existing PAP or bi-level therapy (including adaptive servo-ventilation) in the last 3 months
* Acute cardiac event in the last 3 months
* BMI ≥ 35kg / m²
* Pregnant or lactating women

overallOfficials.name: Olaf Oldenburg, MD
overallOfficials.affiliation: Herz- und Diabeteszentrum NRW, Bad Oeynhausen
